raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/year>, I was the victim of a scam involving criminals impersonating XXXX XXXX. I initially booked a legitimate XXXX ticket  for {$1800.00} on my Citi card. On the day of travel, I could not check in my minor children online and searched XXXX for XXXX customer service number. I unknowingly reached scammers who claimed my childrens booking had failed and demanded a second payment of {$2000.00} to fix the reservation, promising refunds. They emailed me a fake confirmation from a non-XXXX  XXXX email address. 

After my trip, the real XXXX XXXX confirmed : They never received the {$2000.00}. 
They never spoke to me that day. 
My childrens original booking was valid all along. 
The second charge was not associated with them. 

This means the {$2000.00} charge did not go to the merchant shown and was an unauthorized fraudulent transaction. 

Citi has repeatedly misclassified this as a dispute instead of fraud. I have spent months being transferred back and forth between Citis XXXX and XXXX departments, with each department insisting the other is responsible. I requested the evidence Citi used to deny my claim and a clear explanation of their classification decision, but they did not provide it. Their investigation was incomplete and did not consider the airlines statements or the fake email domain used by the scammers. 

I am requesting the CFPBs assistance to require Citi to properly treat this as an unauthorized fraudulent transaction, reopen the claim, and credit the account.
